title: Check if Geometric
text: A geometric series has a constant ratio between consecutive terms. Here $$\frac{9}{10}$$ $$=$$ $$0.9$$ but $$\frac{8}{9}$$ ≈ $$0.889$$ — the ratio is not constant. This is an arithmetic series (common difference $$d$$ $$=$$ -1).

title: Can an arithmetic series have an infinite sum?
text: For the infinite sum S $$=$$ $$a_1$$ / (1 - r) to apply, the series must be geometric with |r| < $$1$$. Arithmetic series with non-zero common difference diverge.

title: Conclusion
text: Because this is an arithmetic series, the terms do not shrink to zero. The infinite sum is not defined.
